Hi Katie,

Thanks for checking in again.

Willow's arrival and departure times have been much more consistent, which has been great!  She is such a polite and lovely young woman, and beautiful with our children.

I will be honest, the feedback from my team is that they are still not feeling a huge amount of 'value add' for our children's learning and development (on top of what they already do each and every day).  They are saying that the sessions feel rather repetitive and it doesn't feel 'exciting and energetic' ...which is what they were looking for.  

We have our final gymnastics session for the term next week, and from there I will get my teaching team to vote on whether they would find it beneficial to continue next term or not.  

Whether this program ends up being the right fit for what we are looking for right at this moment or not, I really appreciate the effort you have gone to in seeking our feedback and trying to improve our experience.

Thanks so much,

Kindest Regards,

Hi Maree

I hope you are all having a great week.

We have a new coach who needs to complete an on-site observation as part of our induction process. This session involves attending a center alongside your regular Head coach to observe the program, assist with setup, and participate in warm-ups to better understand our operational standards and role requirements.

I would like to send Selina to shadow your regular coach during their next session. For your records, I have attached her Working With Children Check (WWCC) information to this email.

Please let me know if this works for your schedule or if you have any questions regarding the induction visit.​
image.png​

Kind Regards
Katie Thomas

Hi Maree

We want to ensure we are providing high-quality, engaging sessions for your center. Our goal is to continue doing what works well and adapt any elements that may not be meeting your needs to better suit your center when delivering Mini Monstars sessions.

If you could please respond and let me know how everything is going, it would be greatly appreciated.

Kind Regards
Katie 🙂

Dear  Maree

 It was so nice speaking with you this morning.We are incredibly excited to be running our program within your centre.

To ensure smooth communication moving forward, please note that our accounts team (accounts@minimonstars.com) will handle all invoicing and financial matters. For all other items, including scheduling and session feedback, I will be your primary point of contact.

Regarding our upcoming schedule, please see the confirmed session details below:
 Sparrow Early Learning Sandstone Point

Commencing -17 June 2026 
09:00 AM-09:40 AM
Coaches: Emily-Jane Harvey
Repeats: Weekly
Week Activity:Week Music with Gymnastics
1 x 40min Kindy

We would love for the parents at your centre to stay connected with us, as we offer much more than just our standard incursions. If they are interested in learning more about our program, they can sign up for our email list here: https://www.minimonstars.net/contact.

Additionally, we have developed a dedicated app where children can continue their learning at home or engage in fun sessions during the day. You can find more information about the app here: https://www.minimonstars.net/mini-monstars-app.

As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.

Hi  Monique,

I am very sorry to hear your feedback, as we pride ourselves on the high quality of our programs and their alignment with the ELF.

I appreciate you providing such comprehensive details. To ensure your next session meets your expectations, I will send one of my leaders, Coach Aisa to attend the session with Fanta. They will provide support and ensure the program is being delivered to our required standards, specifically regarding the level of engagement and "upskilling" for the older children and ensure it is meeting your needs.
If there are specific skills you expected the children to learn in these first four weeks, please share them with me. We can then review where those skills fit into the 46 week program and shuffling some more advanced skills forward provided the children are demonstrating the required safety and foundational skills for progression.
I look forward to hearing about the next session .
I have attached Aisa's WWC Blue Card below.
 

Kind Regards,

Katie

GC&E Rochedale South
Jun 18, 2026, 3:05 PM (1 day ago)
to me

Hi Katie,

 

Thanks for your email, and I’m pretty sure that I gave an emailed feedback the last time.

 

Offhand, I’m not impressed that the teacher was late again – arrived 9:40am. While I understand that last week she was still looking for the Centre, but today nothing has changed. We try to get the children organised to be ready for the activity before 9:30am, but this does not seem to matter as your teacher is late.

 

I was also expecting an improvement in the activity, and because you said that she will need to build on the skills of the children. The children in Kindergarten 1 did the same activities as with last week, which were much suited for smaller children. These activities were too easy for the older children. I didn’t see any “upskilling” in terms of what the children could do or explore.

 

It is unlikely that we will continue with the program if we do not see an improvement in the content and/or delivery.  I hope there is more to this gymnastics program apart from balancing bean bags on children’s heads.

 

Thanks again for your support.

 

 

Kind regards,

 

Monique Orendain

Centre Manager

Hi Katie,

 

We need to have Fanta prepare more complex activities for the Kindy 1. The activities were suited for the Toddlers as they had more limited skills, but the Kindy 1 children are older so they need something more stimulating versus jumping through hoops on the ground.

 

It is difficult to assess the program today as we were stuck indoors, and that might have been her constraint too for Fant. What she needs to understand is that we don’t have a big outdoor yard, so that will always be a challenge.

 

Is there a weekly program or focus when she does the session? Something that we can share with the family as well? Thanks.

patterns.prevention:
• Schedule coach quality assessments within 2 weeks to evaluate engagement, energy levels, and consistency across all active centres.

• Implement a transition protocol requiring 4-week notice and overlap training when replacing coaches to prevent service gaps and parent dissatisfaction.

• Audit equipment inventory and program curriculum at each centre against parent feedback to identify and address boring or under-resourced sessions.

• Establish a "coach backup" roster so centres always have immediate replacement coverage if a coach becomes unavailable or underperforms.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres before term breaks to lock in term 3-4 bookings and prevent schedule conflicts with competing programs.

• Schedule quarterly check-ins with long-standing clients (50+ sessions) to understand satisfaction levels and identify concerns before cancellation.

• Develop flexible scheduling options (alternative time slots, hybrid formats) to accommodate centres with timing constraints that conflict with current offerings.

• Create an internal handover package for centres considering in-house programs, positioning it as a transition partnership rather than a lost client (re-engagement opportunity for future terms).

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

Summary of their time with us: 

center was going to have Daniella but she left prior to center commencing so Kelsey allocated egan with a positive reception in February 2026, with Natalie expressing that the center was impressed and interested in exploring additional gross motor skill classes. However, the program subsequently encountered the following timeline of challenges:

Mid-February 2026: We received feedback regarding low engagement and energy during sessions. I personally observed that the coaching lacked the necessary "vibe," noting that Kelsey Jones needed to be more proactive in engaging the younger age groups.
end of Feb-March Progress was significantly hindered by a gastro outbreak at the center that lasted over three weeks, leading to missed sessions and a lack of consistent feedback.
  In response to engagement concerns, we implemented numerous training options and opportunities for Kelsey to improve her coaching delivery. Despite these interventions, performance remained an issue, ultimately resulting in Kelsey parting ways with Mini Monstars due to these ongoing performance concerns.

Despite my efforts to follow up on quality concerns throughout March and April, communication from the center remained limited. The center formally requested to cease participation in Mini Monstars on April 9, 2026. During a follow-up call on April 15, 2026, Natalie confirmed the cancellation was due to the sessions not meeting quality expectations regarding child engagement.

The center has opted to stop the program for now, though we have left the door open for a potential follow-up in Term 4.
